content:
Is anyone else also experiencing this?

make LLVM=1
  CC      scripts/mod/empty.o
clang-16: error: argument unused during compilation: '-fno-strict-overflow' [-Werror,-Wunused-command-line-argument]
make[2]: *** [scripts/Makefile.build:243: scripts/mod/empty.o] Error 1
make[1]: *** [~/kernel/rust-next/Makefile:1201: prepare0] Error 2
make: *** [Makefile:234: __sub-make] Error 2

Or does someone know a fix?
I have been building with clang 16 since it became default clang on Arch , no issues
Same here. Using clang 16 on debian and no issues.
I'm hitting this issue on arm64 with clang 16

I also had that problem there, Gary told me to try this and it worked:
hardeningDisable = pkgs.linux.hardeningDisable ++ ["strictoverflow"];
where do I put this? in the .nix file? sorry, I'm a nixos noob
do you have a flake for your kernel developing? because I have it in my devshell
I don't have one from what I can tell

I don't know how you would fix it without using flakes...
you can look at my kernel repository and the setup branch, there you should find a flake.nix that works
but if you've never used flakes before, it might be a bit overwhelming (and a lot of the docs arent that good)
I have a .nix file which I load with nix-shell, I'll try adding it there
I have that assignment in the pkgs.mkShell {...} invokation
so if you also have that, it might work
